name: asset_control_evidence_features_v1
description: Row-level evidence features derived via relationships from agents, software inventory, and vulnerability instances. Intended for silver-layer control monitoring and audit evidence readiness.

monitoring_agent_evidence.name: monitoring_agent_evidence
monitoring_agent_evidence.description: Signals that the asset is monitored, how recently, and whether the monitoring surface is healthy.

features.name: has_agent_installed
features.description: True if the asset has at least one associated monitoring agent record (via asset_agent_relationship).
features.purpose: Evidence that endpoint telemetry is in place; used for coverage gating and audit scoping.

features.name: agent_last_checkin_ts
features.description: Most recent agent check-in timestamp for the asset (null if no agent).
features.purpose: Primary freshness signal for monitoring control evidence (detects telemetry gaps).

features.name: agent_health_state
features.description: Normalized health state for the agent (healthy/degraded/offline/unknown) derived from agent telemetry fields.
features.purpose: Used to route remediation tasks (upgrade/reinstall) and to discount confidence in other controls if offline.

features.name: agent_version_age_days
features.description: Age in days of the installed agent version, computed from agent install/update timestamps or version release mapping when available.
features.purpose: Control drift signal; older agents correlate with telemetry gaps and missed detections.

baseline_security_controls.name: baseline_security_controls
baseline_security_controls.description: Evidence that baseline endpoint controls are present (EDR, AV, encryption proxies).

features.name: has_edr_installed
features.description: True if dev_software_instances indicates an EDR product is installed (vendor allowlist + product signatures).
features.purpose: Core detection/control presence signal used to reduce likelihood in effective models.

features.name: edr_vendor
features.description: Normalized vendor name for the installed EDR (e.g., CrowdStrike, Microsoft, SentinelOne, etc.).
features.purpose: Explains detection posture and enables vendor-specific control checks.

features.name: antivirus_present
features.description: True if software inventory indicates AV/endpoint protection is present (vendor/product signatures).
features.purpose: Baseline malware protection evidence; used as a compensating control in likelihood scoring.

features.name: disk_encryption_enabled_proxy
features.description: Proxy indicator for disk encryption derived from software inventory signals (e.g., BitLocker/FileVault presence) and OS family.
features.purpose: Supports confidentiality safeguards (especially HIPAA) and reduces impact of endpoint compromise.

control_effectiveness_vuln_signals.name: control_effectiveness_vuln_signals
control_effectiveness_vuln_signals.description: Asset-level vulnerability evidence used to measure control effectiveness and exposure (still one row per asset).

features.name: has_kev_vuln_open
features.description: True if the asset has at least one open vulnerability instance that matches CISA KEV (join by CVE).
features.purpose: High-confidence exploitation likelihood signal used for urgent prioritization and control deficiency evidence.

features.name: highest_cvss_open
features.description: Maximum CVSS score among open vulnerability instances for the asset (null if none).
features.purpose: Severity exposure signal; used in likelihood weighting and risk tiering.

features.name: exploitable_vuln_present
features.description: True if the asset has an open vulnerability with exploit evidence (ExploitDB/known exploit indicator) OR KEV match.
features.purpose: Likelihood escalator used to trigger urgent workflows and override baseline scoring.
